input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

```powershell
# Needed for WinUI/Windows App SDK support
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required VS components:

```powershell
# Discover available component IDs
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  export --channelId VisualStudio.17.Release

# Modify installation with required workloads
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet
```

## 4. Install Android SDK (via command-line)

```powershell
# Install OpenJDK (required by Android SDK)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Install Android command-line tools
$androidHome = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $androidHome
$cmdLineToolsUrl = "https://dl.google.com/android/repository/commandlinetools-win-latest.zip"  
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $cmdLineToolsUrl -OutFile "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip"
Expand-Archive "$env:TEMP\cmdline-tools.zip" -DestinationPath "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp"
# Google expects a versioned folder structure
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest"
Move-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp\cmdline-tools\*" "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\"
Remove-Item "$androidHome\cmdline-tools-tmp" -Recurse

# Set environment variables
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidHome, "User")
$sdkManager = "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
```

## 5. Discover & Install Required Android Packages

```powershell
# List all available packages (find latest platform/build-tools)
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Sort-Object
& $sdkManager --list | Select-String "build-tools;" | Sort-Object

# Check what MAUI needs (look at the workload manifest)
dotnet workload update --print-download-urls 2>&1 | Select-String "android"

# Install packages (adjust versions from discovery above)
& $sdkManager --sdk_root="$androidHome" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkManager --licenses
```

## 6. (Optional) Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
$avdManager = "$androidHome\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"
& $avdManager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## 7. Enable Windows Developer Mode

```powershell
# Required for deploying Windows (WinUI3) apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 8. Verify Everything Works

```powershell
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI &
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

---

**Key dynamic-discovery tips:**
- `dotnet workload search` — finds current workload names as they evolve
- `sdkmanager --list` — shows all available Android API levels/build-tools
- `dotnet new maui --list` — confirms template availability
- Check `dotnet --info` for target framework monikers if version numbers change

Adjust `net9.0-*` to whatever `dotnet --version` reports (e.g., `net10.0-*` for .NET 10).
